questions.question: What is the topic for Week 17?
questions.explanation: The topic explicitly listed for Lesson Seventeen is STAY PURE.

questions.question: Which of the following was the topic for Week 16?
questions.explanation: Destiny Destroyers was the topic for Week 16.

questions.question: For this is the will of God, your sanctification: that you should abstain from ________.
questions.explanation: The memory verse (1 Thessalonians 4:3) says 'abstain from sexual immorality'.

questions.question: Provide the book, chapter, and verse for this week's memory verse.
questions.explanation: The memory verse reference is 1 Thessalonians 4:3.

questions.question: Type out the memory verse for Week 17 in full (NKJV).
questions.answer: For this is the will of God, your sanctification: that you should abstain from sexual immorality.
questions.explanation: Full text recall of 1 Thessalonians 4:3.

questions.question: According to the memory verse (1 Thessalonians 4:3), what is the will of God regarding your sanctification?
options: A. That you should fast twice a week
B. That you should abstain from sexual immorality
C. That you should give all you have to the poor
D. That you should pray without ceasing
questions.explanation: The verse states explicitly: 'that you should abstain from sexual immorality.'

questions.question: How is 'purity' defined in the introduction?
options: A. A state of being uncontaminated
B. A state of perfect health
questions.explanation: The text states: 'Purity is a state of being uncontaminated.'

questions.question: According to the introduction, which scripture says the 'marriage bed' must be undefiled because God will judge those who stray sexually?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly says 'Hebrews 13:4 says the “marriage bed” must be undefiled...'

questions.question: What is the heading of the first outline?
questions.explanation: Outline I is titled 'OUR ROLES'.

questions.question: According to Outline I, why must we consciously work on our thoughts to stay pure sexually?
options: A. Because our friends can read our minds
B. Because our lives are shaped by them
C. Because God will physically strike us
D. Because we might talk in our sleep
questions.explanation: The text states: 'there is a need to consciously work on our thoughts because our lives are shaped by them.'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to support that 'As gatekeepers, we must guard our hearts'?
questions.explanation: The text says 'As gatekeepers, we must guard our hearts (Proverbs 4:23)...'

questions.question: According to the lesson, since the eyes are gates to the mind, what must they be trained to do?
options: A. Look away from all human beings
B. Focus on what is pure and edifying
D. Close whenever walking past strangers
questions.explanation: The text says 'the eyes... must be trained to focus on what is pure and edifying...'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited regarding the rule that 'our lips must not kiss strangers'?
questions.explanation: The text says 'our lips must not kiss “strangers” (Proverbs 22:14).'

questions.question: The lesson advises staying away from toxic friends because they will be negative influences on us.
questions.explanation: The text says 'We also need to stay off toxic friends (1 Corinthians 15:33) who will be negative influences on us.'

questions.question: Which verses are cited to support renewing our minds with God's word?
questions.explanation: The text cites '(Psalm 119:9; Romans 12:2)' for renewing our minds.

questions.question: What is the heading of the second outline?
questions.explanation: Outline II is titled 'BENEFITS OF SEXUAL PURITY'.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT listed as a benefit of sexual purity in Outline II?
options: A. Freedom from the yoke of bondage
B. Peace with God and with oneself
D. Being audacious with honour and confidence
questions.explanation: Financial prosperity is not listed. The others are (no bondage, peace, audacious/confidence).

questions.question: According to the lesson, sexual sin is equated with slavery.
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states: 'sexual sin is slavery (Proverbs 6:26; Galatians 5:1).'

questions.question: Which two biblical figures are listed as examples of victims because of their lack of sexual purity?
questions.explanation: The text states 'They are not victims like Reuben and Amnon (Genesis 49:3-4; 2 Samuel 13:32).'

questions.question: Who is listed as a biblical figure that made the choice to stay sexually pure and was elevated in Egypt?
questions.explanation: The text says 'Joseph made this choice and he was elevated in Egypt (Genesis 41:40-41).'

questions.question: According to the lesson, what happens to the heart of those who are free from sexual sin?
options: B. The heart is not racing because of guilt and secret sin
D. The heart turns to stone
questions.explanation: The text states 'the heart is not racing because of guilt and secret sin (Psalm 51:3; Isaiah 48:22).'

questions.question: Which prophet discloses that those who bear the vessels of the Lord must be pure?
questions.explanation: The text states: 'The prophet Isaiah discloses that those who bear the vessels of the Lord must be pure (Isaiah 52:11).'

questions.question: What is the final instruction given in the conclusion of the lesson?
options: A. Repent from all your sins immediately
B. Make the right choice to be sexually pure
C. Avoid all members of the opposite sex
questions.explanation: The conclusion explicitly states: 'Make the right choice to be sexually pure.'

questions.question: Where is this verse found? 'Finally then, brethren, we urge and exhort in the Lord Jesus that you should abound more and more...'
questions.verse_text: Finally then, brethren, we urge and exhort in the Lord Jesus that you should abound more and more...
questions.explanation: This is verse 1 from the main Bible Passage.

questions.question: According to the Bible Passage (1 Thessalonians 4:4), what should each of you know how to do?
options: A. How to preach the gospel
B. How to possess his own vessel in sanctification and honor
C. How to heal the sick
D. How to cast out demons
questions.explanation: Verse 4 reads 'that each of you should know how to possess his own vessel in sanctification and honor'.

questions.question: In 1 Thessalonians 4:5, what group of people is mentioned as 'who do not know God' and act 'in passion of lust'?
questions.explanation: Verse 5 says 'not in passion of lust, like the Gentiles who do not know God'.

questions.question: According to 1 Thessalonians 4:2, the brethren already knew the commandments that were given to them through the Lord Jesus.
questions.explanation: Verse 2 explicitly states: 'for you know what commandments we gave you through the Lord Jesus.'

questions.question: Our hands must be kept clean and not allowed to touch what is ________ or immoral.
questions.explanation: The text says 'touch what is obscene or immoral (Psalm 24:4a).'

questions.question: What happens if we listen to erotic words, songs, etc., according to the lesson's section on the ears?
options: B. It can trigger sexual pollution
C. It will make us wealthy
D. It gives us a headache
questions.explanation: The text says 'because listening to erotic words, songs, etc. can trigger sexual pollution.'

questions.question: Staying pure sexually helps our relationship with God because sexual sin breaks our association with God.
questions.explanation: The text says 'It helps our relationship with God; sexual sin breaks our association with God...'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to show that those who are sexually pure are audacious?
questions.explanation: The text says 'Those who are sexually pure are audacious (Proverbs 28:1).'

questions.question: Which verse is cited for the rule that 'our feet must be guided to go only to the places that honour God'?
questions.explanation: The text lists '(Psalm 119:101)' right after the instruction about feet.

questions.question: To stay pure, there is no need to ask God for help as long as you discipline your mind.
questions.explanation: The lesson ends Outline I with 'It is also necessary to ask God to help us.'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to support that sexual purity is honourable?
questions.explanation: The text says 'It is honourable (Esther 2:2).'

questions.question: According to the introduction, what is the 'marriage bed'?
options: B. It is a place of rest
C. It is the altar of the church
questions.explanation: The intro says 'Hebrews 13:4 says the “marriage bed” must be undefiled...'
